sglang.title: [HiCache] Support heterogeneous tp for hicache storage
sglang.author: huangtingwei9988
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
HiCacheStorage supports sharing key-value caches between Sglang instances of different tp sizes.
need to pass the least common multiple of tp_sizes. For example, if tp_size=1,2,4 (three instances), the least common multiple is 4.
--hicache-storage-backend-extra-config '{"tp_lcm_size":4}'

sglang.title: [sgl-kernel][Feat][B200][2/N] Support MXFP8 Grouped GEMM in Blackwell
sglang.author: HydraQYH
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Follow #13731. The MXFP8 Group Quant Kernel uses a group-wise schedule. This scheduling strategy sometimes results in an unbalanced load. In this PR, we use a flatten schedule. Let's illustrate this with a concrete example:
Group-wise Schedule:
Group 0: CTA 0-> Tile 0, CTA 1-> Tile 1, ...

sglang.title: [Fix] Skip signal handler registration when not on main thread
sglang.author: xyuzh
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
When launch_server is called from a background thread (e.g., inside a Ray actor that runs the server in a threading.Thread), Python raises ValueError because signal.signal() can only be called from the main thread.
Changes
Guard the SIGQUIT handler registration in _set_envs_and_config wit

sglang.title: Fix NSA CP positions mismatch in eagle NextN model
sglang.author: alisonshao
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

When context parallelism (CP) is enabled, the eagle NextN model (deepseek_nextn.py) splits hidden_states across CP ranks but does not split positions, causing a shape mismatch in the NSA indexer's rotary embedding
The rotary embedding uses positions.size(0) as batch size to reshape k_rope,

Purpose

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix Harmony preamble visibility in Responses API

vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Per the Harmony spec, preambles (commentary channel messages with no recipient) are "intended to be shown to end-users". vLLM was incorrectly treating them as hidden reasoning across multiple code paths.
This PR fixes preamble visibility in 6 code paths across 3 files:
Parser (harmony_utils.

Purpose
The tests in test_punica_ops can fail if a previous test ran and changed the device that kernels execute in, in particular Triton kernels, by using torch.cuda.set_device, e.g. lora/test_layers.py.  This can cause subsequent tests to also fail, since the system seems to go into a bad state on

vllm.title: fix(mxfp4): Disable monolithic path for TRITON backend with EP
vllm.author: elizabetht
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Problem
When running MXFP4 models with the TRITON backend and expert parallelism (EP) enabled (e.g. vllm serve openai/gpt-oss-20b -tp=2 -ep), the server crashes with an illegal memory access.

vllm.title: [ROCm][CI] Extending attention backend coverage for Eagle spec decode tests
vllm.author: AndreasKaratzas
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR fixes the attention backend handling in test_eagle_correctness for ROCm platforms:
Previously, the test would skip entirely when running Llama-4-Scout with FLASH_ATTN on ROCm. Instead of skipping, we now fall back to the FLEX_ATTENTION backend explicitly, allowing the test to actually run on

This mirror was deleted accidentally from:
#34923
This PR reverts that.

vllm.title: [offloader] v2: Hide weight onloading latency via prefetching
vllm.author: minosfuture
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
This PR adds CPU weight offloader that hides weight onloading latency by prefetching weights.  This saves the performance cost of zero-copy UVA access. This technique was first developed in SGLang for GB200: https://lmsys.org/blog/2025-09-25-gb200-part-2/, and now adapted to support torch.co

vllm.title: [UX] Add `--performance-mode {balanced,interactivity,throughput}`
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Adds a new flag to specify user intent for workload, --performance-mode {balanced,interactivity,throughput}.
Default is balanced which preserves existing behavior.
To start and just demonstrate the feature, setting interactivity mode captures fine-grained CUDA graphs at small batch sizes (st

vllm.title: [UX] Add `--moe-backend` arg for explicit kernel selection
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Adds --moe-backend argument for explicit MoE kernel selection, allowing users to override the automatic backend selection logic (e.g., --moe-backend triton, --moe-backend marlin, --moe-backend flashinfer_trtllm)
Supports all three oracle paths currently implemented: unquantized, FP8, and NVF

Fixes AttributeError: 'int' object has no attribute 'index' in SMControlContextManager.__init__() when running with data parallel and expert parallel enabled (DBO tests).

vllm.title: openpangu-vl support video input
vllm.author: hujiaxin0
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
This is a method for calculating the Pangu video sampling frame, which is different from the existing method. total_frames_num indicates the total number of frames. It is more reasonable to start the calculation from frame 0 corresponding to 0 seconds.
Assume that total_num_frames = 10 and f

vllm.title: [Kernel] Refactor FlashInfer allreduce for mnnvl backend

Purpose
Flashinfer has a new allreduce API: flashinfer-ai/flashinfer#2130
and a mnnvl backend for allreduce optimized for multi-node NVLink cases (flashinfer-ai/flashinfer#1213).
The mnnvl backend performs same/better than the old trtllm backend that vLLM currently uses.

PyTorch / KleidiAI kernels natively support the following configs (with inputs that get dynamically quantized to int8):

channelwise with bfloat16 / float32 activation
groupwise with float32 activations

groupwise with bfloat16/float16 activations: we need to upcast activations
